name: HSIL RISCOS Draft Knowledge Bundle
description: Draft evidence-oriented bundle translated from the HSIL - RISCOS.csv table. It captures provenance, evidence statements, and only the candidate rules that the current Phase 0 DSL can express safely.

source_documents.title: Malik S et al. (2025) PMID: 41333356
source_documents.notes: Oncology ICU cohort. Nosocomial definition: HAP/VAP >48h post-admission/intubation. Source table: HSIL - RISCOS.csv.

source_documents.title: Jimenez-Juarez (2025) DOI: 10.3390/v17060771
source_documents.notes: Pediatric tertiary cohort. Source table notes paradoxical breathing as a code-blue trigger and immediate MV predictor.

source_documents.title: HA-RVI Study (2025) DOI: 10.1111/tid.70135
source_documents.notes: Hospital-acquired respiratory viral infection study with >=72h nosocomial threshold and oncology/SCT ward focus.

source_documents.title: Fan et al. (2025) PMID: 39912067
source_documents.notes: HA-DRP study. Useful mainly as evidence for risk modelling, not directly executable rules in the current DSL.

source_documents.title: Liddy et al. (2025) DOI: 10.1097/CCE.1289
source_documents.notes: HA-sepsis study. Useful for evidence statements around strain and mortality; not yet cleanly executable in the current DSL.

source_documents.title: Bonin et al. (2025) DOI: 10.57187/s.4324
source_documents.notes: Nosocomial respiratory virus cohort with >=48h threshold and quadrivalent PCR focus.

source_documents.title: Ursic et al. (2025) DOI: 10.3390/jcm13226458
source_documents.notes: Mixed ICU cohort comparing rapid PCR to culture. Good source for diagnostic-lag evidence.

source_documents.title: Zhang et al. (2025) DOI: 10.3389/fpubh.2025.1495794
source_documents.notes: HA-influenza risk modelling study with >=48h onset threshold and XGBoost-based screening suggestions.

source_documents.title: Costa Salachas et al. (2025) PMID: 40217353
source_documents.notes: Architectural transmission study highlighting double-occupancy rooms and hospital-acquired viral incidence.

source_documents.title: InfoGripe / FIOCRUZ (2024/2025)
source_documents.notes: Regional seasonality and resource-strain source for Brazil. Not directly executable in the current local fact model.

source_documents.title: Dabaja-Younis et al. (2024) DOI: 10.1186/s12879-024-09240-0
source_documents.notes: Behavioral compliance and protocol-automation evidence source.

source_documents.title: Grant et al. (2025) DOI: 10.1093/cid/ciae558
source_documents.notes: Comparative HA-influenza vs HA-COVID study with >=3 days post-admission definition.

pathogen_packs.notes: Draft pathogen pack derived from the HSIL risk table. Only rules that map to the current DSL should be treated as executable.

evidence_statements.statement: In oncology ICU settings, nosocomial viral pneumonia and sepsis after 48 hours are associated with high ventilator demand and strong resource strain.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Table combines influenza and broader viral pneumonia observations.

evidence_statements.statement: Paradoxical breathing is an actionable escalation marker in pediatric viral respiratory admissions and can serve as an immediate review trigger.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Requires a clinical sign fact that does not yet exist in the current fact bridge.

evidence_statements.statement: Hospital-acquired respiratory viral infection identified at or after 72 hours on oncology or SCT wards is associated with greater life-support use and should trigger ward-level vigilance.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Requires ward specialty and chemotherapy recency facts for stronger implementation.

evidence_statements.statement: Albumin, cholinesterase, age, indwelling devices, and ventilator use are relevant risk features for hospital-acquired deterioration, but they currently fit a scoring model better than the minimal rule DSL.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Better suited to future feature engineering and scoring infrastructure.

evidence_statements.statement: Hospital-acquired sepsis involving influenza or pneumonia imposes high critical-care and resource strain, supporting governance rules that force review rather than autonomous action.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: The table does not provide a directly executable action threshold.

evidence_statements.statement: Nosocomial respiratory detections after 48 hours with day-4 deterioration suggest a useful surveillance trigger, but the current DSL lacks a derived day-of-stay deterioration fact.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Requires a future deterioration timeline fact.

evidence_statements.statement: Rapid multiplex PCR significantly shortens diagnostic delay in ICU viral pneumonia and supports review pathways for hidden influenza in culture-negative decline.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Current DSL can store this as evidence but cannot yet compare diagnostic lag times.

evidence_statements.statement: Hospital-acquired influenza after 48 hours with older age, malignancy, chronic renal failure, and pneumonia supports automated high-risk screening.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Most predictor features are not yet exposed in the current fact bridge.

evidence_statements.statement: Double-occupancy rooms are associated with higher hospital-acquired viral incidence and are a strong candidate structural risk signal for ward vulnerability logic.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: This can be expressed if room-type facts are available, but the current demo fact bridge is patient-centric.

evidence_statements.statement: Regional seasonality and surge timing support future seasonal adaptation rules, but these require external bulletin and jurisdiction facts that are not yet in the local fact model.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Best treated as future regional adaptation data rather than immediate executable logic.

evidence_statements.statement: Routine viral threats may not trigger human vigilance consistently, supporting policies that force logged review workflows for influenza-related actions.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: This is governance evidence, not a disease-definition rule.

evidence_statements.statement: Hospital-acquired influenza can carry critical-care demand and mortality comparable to modern hospital-acquired COVID cohorts, supporting equal-severity review treatment.
evidence_statements.uncertainty_note: Comparable severity does not itself define an executable threshold in the current DSL.

rule_artifacts.name: Hospital-onset influenza over 48 hours
rule_artifacts.description: Candidate rule derived from multiple HSIL rows using the most common nosocomial threshold in the table.
rule_artifacts.uncertainty_note: The table contains multiple thresholds, including 48h, 72h, 96h, and 5 days.

rule_artifacts.name: Double-room structural risk signal
rule_artifacts.description: Candidate rule expressing double-room occupancy as a structural viral risk factor.
rule_artifacts.uncertainty_note: Useful only when room type is reliably mapped into canonical facts.

rule_artifacts.name: Review influenza with equal-severity treatment
rule_artifacts.description: Candidate workflow rule for hospital-onset influenza classifications from the HSIL draft pack.
rule_artifacts.uncertainty_note: This rule reflects governance response, not an automated clinical order.

rule_artifacts.name: Medication-related influenza actions remain review only
rule_artifacts.description: Candidate governance rule supported by strain and stewardship evidence in the table.

rule_artifacts.name: All surfaced influenza actions must be logged
rule_artifacts.description: Candidate governance rule derived from compliance and protocol automation findings.

action_catalog.description: Human review of whether hospital-acquired influenza requires the same high-severity operational response used for comparable modern respiratory viral threats.
